metric_value: 'Hard drive speed counts for an awful lot of overall computing responsiveness' — Peter Kastner, EVP and Chief Research Officer, Aberdeen Group, to NewsFactor
notes: Prescient observation — validated by SSD transition 2012+

metric_value: 'Get a good laptop, docking station, external monitor and a few more things... and then ditch your desktop'
Designers of mobile disk drives face unique constraints: less space, less power, more shock-resistant than desktop HDDs; disk drives are the most noticeable lagging mobile component
notes: From NewsFactor excerpt via Smallbiz reblog

metric_value: Ray predicts users can ditch desktops once laptops + docking + external displays reach parity

metric_name: US laptops overtake desktops in unit shipments
metric_value: Q2 2008: US consumer notebook shipments exceeded desktop shipments for the first time (Gartner/IDC data). Laptop-as-primary-device pattern became mainstream ~5-6 years after Ray's prediction.
By 2015 majority of new consumer laptops shipped with SSDs; by 2018 SSDs were the default. The shift was driven by exactly the I/O-bottleneck reasoning Kastner articulated in 2002.
notes: Kastner's HDD-bottleneck framing directly supports SSD thesis
